standards.title: Human Resource Planning
standards.description: The organization conducts systematic human resource planning aligned with its strategic goals to ensure adequate staffing across all departments.

objective_elements.description: A documented human resource plan exists that aligns staffing requirements with the organization's strategic direction and service scope.

measurable_elements.description: The organization maintains a written HR plan that is reviewed periodically and reflects current and projected service needs.

objective_elements.description: Staffing ratios and workforce numbers are determined based on patient volume, acuity levels, and departmental workload data.

measurable_elements.description: Staffing norms are established for each department considering patient census, case complexity, and national benchmarks.

objective_elements.description: Job descriptions are defined for every category of staff, specifying qualifications, duties, reporting relationships, and accountability.

measurable_elements.description: Written job descriptions are available for all positions and include required qualifications, key responsibilities, and reporting structure.

objective_elements.description: Succession planning is undertaken for leadership and key clinical positions to ensure continuity of services.

measurable_elements.description: A succession plan identifying potential successors for critical leadership and clinical roles is documented and periodically updated.

objective_elements.description: Periodic reviews of staffing adequacy are conducted to address gaps and changing organizational requirements.

measurable_elements.description: Staffing adequacy reviews are performed at defined intervals with documented action plans for identified gaps.

objective_elements.description: Budgetary provisions are made for human resource activities including recruitment, training, and staff welfare.

measurable_elements.description: An annual HR budget covering recruitment costs, training programs, and employee welfare is approved and monitored.

standards.title: Recruitment and Selection Process
standards.description: The organization follows a defined and transparent recruitment process ensuring selection of qualified and competent personnel.

objective_elements.description: A documented recruitment and selection process exists covering sourcing, screening, interviewing, and appointment procedures.

measurable_elements.description: Written recruitment policies and procedures define each step from requisition to appointment, including approval authorities.

objective_elements.description: Qualifications, professional registrations, and licensure of all clinical staff are verified before appointment.

measurable_elements.description: Original documents for educational qualifications and current registration with relevant statutory bodies are verified and copies retained.

objective_elements.description: Background verification including reference checks is conducted for all new employees prior to joining.

measurable_elements.description: A background verification process including employment references and character checks is completed before or within the probation period.

objective_elements.description: An equal opportunity and non-discrimination policy guides all hiring decisions.

measurable_elements.description: The recruitment policy incorporates provisions for equal opportunity and prohibits discrimination based on gender, religion, caste, or disability.

objective_elements.description: A structured probation and confirmation process evaluates new employees' suitability for continued employment.

measurable_elements.description: Probationary evaluation criteria are defined and applied consistently, with documented outcomes leading to confirmation or separation.

objective_elements.description: Personnel files are maintained for all staff containing required documentation and are kept secure and confidential.

measurable_elements.description: Individual personnel files with appointment letters, qualification copies, registration certificates, and appraisal records are maintained securely.

standards.title: Credentialing and Privileging of Medical Professionals
standards.description: The organization has a defined process for credentialing and granting clinical privileges to medical professionals based on verified qualifications and competence.

objective_elements.description: A credentialing process is established for all medical staff including physicians, surgeons, and allied health professionals.

measurable_elements.description: A documented credentialing policy specifies the process for verifying qualifications, training, experience, and registration of all medical professionals.

objective_elements.description: Primary source verification of credentials is performed for all clinical staff to confirm the authenticity of qualifications.

measurable_elements.description: Credentials are verified directly with issuing institutions or through recognized verification agencies, with documented evidence of verification.

objective_elements.description: Clinical privileges are granted based on credentials, competence assessment, and scope of services offered by the organization.

measurable_elements.description: A privileging process defines specific clinical procedures and activities each practitioner is authorized to perform, based on verified credentials and demonstrated competence.

objective_elements.description: Periodic re-credentialing is conducted at defined intervals to ensure continued competence and current registration.

measurable_elements.description: Re-credentialing is performed at least every three years or as per organizational policy, reviewing updated registrations, competence, and performance data.

objective_elements.description: A process exists for granting temporary privileges and emergency privileges when clinically necessary.

measurable_elements.description: Policies define the criteria, duration, and approval process for temporary and emergency clinical privileges, including oversight requirements.

objective_elements.description: Credentialing of nursing staff is performed to verify their qualifications, registration, and competence for assigned duties.

measurable_elements.description: Nursing staff credentials including nursing council registration, educational qualifications, and specialized training certificates are verified and documented.

objective_elements.description: A credentialing committee or equivalent body oversees the credentialing and privileging process.

measurable_elements.description: A credentialing committee with defined membership, terms of reference, and meeting frequency reviews and approves all credentialing and privileging decisions.

objective_elements.description: The scope of clinical practice is defined for all categories of clinical staff based on their qualifications and privileges.

measurable_elements.description: Each clinical staff member has a documented scope of practice that is communicated to them and to relevant departments.

standards.title: Staff Orientation Program
standards.description: The organization provides a structured orientation program for all new staff to familiarize them with the organization's policies, facilities, and their specific roles.

objective_elements.description: A documented orientation program is implemented for all newly hired staff before they assume their duties independently.

measurable_elements.description: An orientation program with defined content, duration, and assessment criteria is provided to all new staff with documented completion records.

objective_elements.description: General orientation covers the organization's mission, vision, values, policies, and key procedures applicable to all employees.

measurable_elements.description: General orientation topics include organizational structure, mission and values, HR policies, code of conduct, and emergency procedures.

objective_elements.description: Department-specific orientation familiarizes new staff with their work area, equipment, protocols, and reporting relationships.

measurable_elements.description: Department heads conduct specific orientation covering unit layout, equipment operation, departmental protocols, and team introductions.

objective_elements.description: Orientation includes mandatory training on patient safety, infection control, fire safety, and emergency codes.

measurable_elements.description: All new staff receive mandatory orientation on patient safety goals, hand hygiene, infection prevention, fire safety protocols, and code procedures.

objective_elements.description: Completion of the orientation program is tracked and documented for each new employee.

measurable_elements.description: Orientation attendance and completion records are maintained in personnel files with sign-off by the employee and supervisor.

objective_elements.description: Staff who are transferred or reassigned within the organization receive re-orientation relevant to their new role and department.

measurable_elements.description: A re-orientation process is defined for internally transferred or reassigned staff covering new department procedures and role expectations.

standards.title: Ongoing In-Service Training and Education
standards.description: The organization provides continuous in-service education and training programs to maintain and enhance staff competence and knowledge.

objective_elements.description: A training needs assessment is conducted annually to identify skill gaps and learning requirements across all departments.

measurable_elements.description: An annual training needs assessment process identifies department-level and individual skill gaps using performance data, incident reports, and new technology requirements.

objective_elements.description: A documented training calendar is prepared based on assessed needs and covers both mandatory and developmental programs.

measurable_elements.description: An annual training calendar is developed, approved, and circulated listing all mandatory, departmental, and developmental training programs with schedules.

objective_elements.description: Mandatory training programs on fire safety, basic life support, infection control, and patient safety are conducted for all relevant staff.

measurable_elements.description: All staff receive periodic mandatory training on fire safety, BLS/CPR, infection prevention, safe medication practices, and patient safety with documented attendance.

objective_elements.description: Training effectiveness is evaluated through appropriate methods to assess knowledge retention and skill application.

measurable_elements.description: Post-training evaluations are conducted using assessments, observations, or feedback instruments, and results are used to improve future programs.

objective_elements.description: Training attendance and completion records are maintained for all staff and monitored for compliance.

measurable_elements.description: Training records documenting program name, date, attendees, and completion status are maintained and accessible for review and audit.

objective_elements.description: Staff are provided access to continuing professional education and development opportunities relevant to their discipline.

measurable_elements.description: The organization facilitates continuing education through sponsorships, study leave, conference attendance, and access to professional journals or online learning platforms.

standards.title: Competency Assessment of Staff
standards.description: The organization defines and assesses competencies for all categories of staff to ensure capability in performing assigned duties.

objective_elements.description: A competency framework defining the required knowledge, skills, and attitudes for each category of staff is documented.

measurable_elements.description: Role-specific competency matrices are developed defining required clinical, technical, and behavioral competencies for each staff category.

objective_elements.description: Initial competency assessment is performed at the time of hiring or role assignment to verify baseline capabilities.

measurable_elements.description: New hires and newly assigned staff undergo initial competency evaluation using practical demonstrations, written tests, or clinical assessments.

objective_elements.description: Ongoing competency evaluation is conducted at defined intervals to ensure sustained capability and identify development needs.

measurable_elements.description: Periodic competency re-assessments are conducted annually or as defined, using observation, case reviews, simulation, or skill demonstration methods.

objective_elements.description: Identified competency gaps are addressed through targeted training, mentoring, or supervised practice with follow-up evaluation.

measurable_elements.description: Competency gap remediation plans are developed for staff who do not meet expected levels, with timelines and reassessment criteria.

objective_elements.description: Competency verification is mandatory for staff performing high-risk, high-volume, or problem-prone procedures.

measurable_elements.description: Staff performing high-risk or specialized procedures are assessed for specific procedural competency with documented sign-off by authorized evaluators.

objective_elements.description: All competency assessments and their outcomes are documented and maintained in staff records.

measurable_elements.description: Competency assessment records including evaluation tools, scores, dates, and assessor details are filed in personnel records and are retrievable for review.

standards.title: Performance Appraisal System
standards.description: The organization implements a systematic performance appraisal process for all categories of staff linked to job expectations and professional development.

objective_elements.description: A defined performance appraisal system outlines the methodology, frequency, and criteria for evaluating staff performance.

measurable_elements.description: A performance appraisal policy defines the appraisal methodology, rating scales, review frequency, and roles of appraiser and reviewer.

objective_elements.description: Performance reviews are conducted at defined intervals for all categories of staff including clinical, administrative, and support personnel.

measurable_elements.description: Periodic performance appraisals are completed for all staff categories at intervals not exceeding one year, with documented evidence.

objective_elements.description: Performance evaluation criteria are linked to job descriptions, competency expectations, and organizational objectives.

measurable_elements.description: Appraisal criteria reflect the key result areas from job descriptions, required competencies, and contribution to department and organizational goals.

objective_elements.description: Constructive feedback is provided during appraisals and an individual development plan is created to address identified areas of improvement.

measurable_elements.description: Performance feedback discussions include strengths, areas for improvement, and mutually agreed development plans with timelines.

objective_elements.description: Appraisal records are maintained confidentially and are accessible to authorized personnel only.

measurable_elements.description: Completed appraisal forms, development plans, and feedback records are securely stored in personnel files with restricted access.

standards.title: Staff Health and Safety Program
standards.description: The organization implements a comprehensive health and safety program to protect staff from occupational health risks and promote well-being.

objective_elements.description: Pre-employment health screening is conducted for all new staff to establish baseline health status and fitness for duty.

measurable_elements.description: All new employees undergo a pre-employment medical examination including relevant tests based on their job role, with fitness certification documented.

objective_elements.description: Periodic health assessments are conducted for staff at defined intervals, with particular focus on those in high-risk areas.

measurable_elements.description: Annual or periodic health check-ups are provided to all staff, with enhanced screening frequency for those working in high-risk environments.

objective_elements.description: An immunization program is implemented for healthcare workers based on their exposure risk and national guidelines.

measurable_elements.description: Hepatitis B, influenza, and other recommended vaccinations are provided to at-risk healthcare workers with immunization records maintained.

objective_elements.description: Post-exposure prophylaxis protocols are established and accessible for needle-stick injuries and exposure to blood-borne pathogens.

measurable_elements.description: PEP protocols for needle-stick and sharps injuries are documented, accessible 24/7, and include reporting procedures, baseline testing, and follow-up schedules.

objective_elements.description: Staff injuries and illnesses are reported, documented, and managed through a defined process with appropriate follow-up.

measurable_elements.description: A staff injury reporting system captures all workplace injuries and illnesses, triggers investigation, provides treatment, and tracks recovery outcomes.

objective_elements.description: Workplace safety measures are implemented to prevent injuries including ergonomic assessments and safe work practices.

measurable_elements.description: Workplace safety measures including safe lifting techniques, ergonomic workstation design, and anti-slip flooring are implemented and monitored.

objective_elements.description: Mental health and psychological well-being support is available for staff including stress management and counseling services.

measurable_elements.description: Employee assistance programs, counseling services, and stress management initiatives are available and promoted to support staff mental health and well-being.

standards.title: Occupational Hazard Management
standards.description: The organization identifies, evaluates, and controls occupational hazards to protect healthcare workers from workplace-related health risks.

objective_elements.description: Occupational hazards across all work areas are systematically identified, assessed, and documented with risk ratings.

measurable_elements.description: A hazard identification and risk assessment process is conducted for all work areas with documented risk registers and mitigation plans.

objective_elements.description: Safety measures for staff exposed to ionizing and non-ionizing radiation are implemented in compliance with regulatory requirements.

measurable_elements.description: Radiation safety measures including dosimetry monitoring, protective shielding, exposure limits, and AERB compliance are implemented for all radiation workers.

objective_elements.description: Chemical safety measures including availability of material safety data sheets are in place for all hazardous substances used.

measurable_elements.description: MSDS/SDS documents are available at point of use for all chemicals, and staff are trained on safe handling, storage, and spill management procedures.

objective_elements.description: Biological hazard management including safe handling of infectious materials and biomedical waste is implemented.

measurable_elements.description: Standard precautions for biological hazard management, biomedical waste segregation, and safe handling of potentially infectious materials are followed and monitored.

objective_elements.description: Ergonomic risk assessments are conducted and corrective measures implemented to prevent musculoskeletal disorders.

measurable_elements.description: Ergonomic assessments cover workstation design, manual handling activities, and repetitive tasks with corrective actions documented and implemented.

objective_elements.description: Appropriate personal protective equipment is provided, and its use is ensured for all staff exposed to occupational hazards.

measurable_elements.description: PPE appropriate to the hazard type is procured, made available, and its correct usage is enforced and monitored through audits and training.

standards.title: Employee Welfare and Grievance Redressal
standards.description: The organization promotes employee welfare and has mechanisms for addressing grievances and ensuring a supportive work environment.

objective_elements.description: Employee welfare programs addressing social, recreational, and financial well-being are implemented.

measurable_elements.description: Welfare programs including insurance, recreational activities, financial assistance schemes, and recognition awards are documented and available to all staff.

objective_elements.description: A formal grievance redressal mechanism exists for employees to raise concerns and have them addressed in a timely manner.

measurable_elements.description: A grievance redressal policy defines the process for filing complaints, investigation steps, timelines for resolution, and escalation procedures.

objective_elements.description: An anti-harassment policy including Prevention of Sexual Harassment (POSH) compliance is in place with an internal complaints committee.

measurable_elements.description: A POSH-compliant anti-harassment policy is documented, an Internal Complaints Committee is constituted, and awareness training is conducted for all staff.

objective_elements.description: Adequate staff amenities including rest areas, changing rooms, and cafeteria facilities are provided.

measurable_elements.description: Staff amenities such as rest rooms, lockers, changing areas, clean drinking water, and food services are available and maintained in hygienic condition.

objective_elements.description: Employee satisfaction surveys are conducted periodically and results are analyzed to drive improvement initiatives.

measurable_elements.description: Employee satisfaction surveys are conducted at least annually, results are analyzed for trends, and action plans are developed and tracked for identified concerns.

standards.title: Management of Volunteers
standards.description: The organization has a structured approach to managing volunteers including their selection, training, supervision, and scope of activities.

objective_elements.description: A documented policy governs the engagement and management of volunteers within the organization.

measurable_elements.description: A volunteer management policy defines eligibility criteria, enrollment procedures, scope of permitted activities, and code of conduct for volunteers.

objective_elements.description: Volunteers are screened and selected through a defined process including background checks and health screening.

measurable_elements.description: A screening process for volunteers includes identity verification, background checks, health screening, and documentation before engagement.

objective_elements.description: Volunteers receive orientation and training relevant to their assigned roles including patient safety and infection control basics.

measurable_elements.description: Orientation and role-specific training are provided to volunteers covering organizational policies, patient safety basics, infection prevention, and confidentiality.

objective_elements.description: Roles and responsibilities of volunteers are clearly defined, limiting their involvement to non-clinical support activities.

measurable_elements.description: Written role descriptions for volunteers clearly delineate permitted activities, prohibited tasks, and boundaries to ensure patient safety.

objective_elements.description: Volunteer activities are supervised by designated staff and their performance is periodically evaluated.

measurable_elements.description: Designated supervisors oversee volunteer activities, provide guidance, and conduct periodic evaluations of volunteer engagement and conduct.

standards.title: Management of Outsourced and Contracted Staff
standards.description: The organization ensures that outsourced and contracted staff meet defined competency and compliance standards equivalent to employed staff.

objective_elements.description: A policy governs the engagement, management, and performance oversight of outsourced and contracted staff.

measurable_elements.description: A documented policy for outsourced staff management covers vendor selection criteria, service level agreements, and performance monitoring mechanisms.

objective_elements.description: Credentials and competencies of outsourced clinical staff are verified to the same standards as employed clinical staff.

measurable_elements.description: Outsourced clinical staff undergo credential verification including qualification checks, registration validity, and competency assessment before deployment.

objective_elements.description: Outsourced staff receive appropriate orientation to the organization's policies, safety procedures, and their work area.

measurable_elements.description: Outsourced staff orientation covers organizational policies, patient safety, infection prevention, emergency procedures, and specific departmental protocols.

objective_elements.description: Performance of outsourced staff is monitored against defined service level agreements and quality parameters.

measurable_elements.description: Regular performance reviews of outsourced services are conducted against SLA benchmarks, with documented feedback and corrective actions.

objective_elements.description: Contractual agreements with outsourcing agencies include provisions for compliance with organizational quality and safety standards.

measurable_elements.description: Contracts with outsourcing vendors include clauses on quality standards, safety compliance, credential verification, confidentiality, and penalty provisions.

standards.title: Nursing Staff Excellence and Development
standards.description: The organization promotes nursing excellence through defined leadership structures, adequate staffing, professional development, and quality monitoring.

objective_elements.description: A nursing leadership structure with defined authority and accountability is established within the organization.

measurable_elements.description: A nursing organizational chart defines the leadership hierarchy, reporting relationships, and authority levels from unit in-charge to chief nursing officer.

objective_elements.description: Nurse staffing ratios are determined for each clinical area based on patient acuity, census, and evidence-based norms.

measurable_elements.description: Nurse-to-patient ratios are defined for general wards, ICUs, emergency, and specialized units based on acuity and national staffing guidelines.

objective_elements.description: Nursing care standards and clinical protocols are documented, implemented, and periodically updated.

measurable_elements.description: Nursing standard operating procedures and clinical protocols covering assessment, intervention, documentation, and evaluation are maintained and reviewed annually.

objective_elements.description: Professional development programs specific to nursing staff are implemented to enhance clinical skills and career advancement.

measurable_elements.description: Nursing-specific training programs including clinical skill workshops, specialty certifications, and leadership development are offered and participation is tracked.

objective_elements.description: Nursing quality indicators are monitored, analyzed, and used to drive improvements in patient care.

measurable_elements.description: Key nursing quality indicators such as fall rates, pressure injury incidence, medication errors, and patient satisfaction are tracked, trended, and benchmarked.

standards.title: Health Information Management Planning
standards.description: The organization plans and implements a comprehensive health information management system to support clinical care, management decisions, and regulatory compliance.

objective_elements.description: A documented health information management policy defines the scope, objectives, and governance of information management activities.

measurable_elements.description: A health information management policy covering data governance, information lifecycle, and quality objectives is approved and communicated to relevant staff.

objective_elements.description: Designated responsibility for health information management is assigned to qualified personnel or a department.

measurable_elements.description: A qualified individual or department is designated to manage health information with defined roles, responsibilities, and authority for information governance.

objective_elements.description: Standardized data collection methods are established across all departments to ensure consistency and comparability.

measurable_elements.description: Data collection formats, definitions, and coding standards are standardized across departments to enable meaningful aggregation and comparison.

objective_elements.description: Data aggregation and analysis mechanisms are in place to transform raw data into actionable information for decision-making.

measurable_elements.description: Systems and processes for data aggregation, statistical analysis, and report generation are established and used by departments for monitoring and improvement.

objective_elements.description: Information flow pathways are defined across departments to ensure timely availability of relevant data to authorized users.

measurable_elements.description: Information flow diagrams mapping data sources, destinations, frequency, and responsible persons are documented for key organizational processes.

objective_elements.description: Data validation processes are implemented to ensure accuracy, completeness, and reliability of collected information.

measurable_elements.description: Data validation checks including completeness verification, range checks, and consistency reviews are performed at defined points in data entry workflows.

objective_elements.description: Health information is systematically used for evidence-based decision-making at clinical, operational, and strategic levels.

measurable_elements.description: Evidence of data-driven decision-making is demonstrated through documented use of analytics in quality improvement, resource planning, and clinical governance forums.

standards.title: Medical Records Management
standards.description: The organization maintains accurate, complete, and accessible medical records for every patient encounter to support continuity of care and legal compliance.

objective_elements.description: A standardized medical record format is defined that includes all essential clinical documentation components.

measurable_elements.description: A standardized medical record format with defined sections for history, examination, diagnosis, treatment plan, progress notes, and discharge summary is in use.

objective_elements.description: Medical records are completed in a timely manner with entries made contemporaneously during or immediately after patient encounters.

measurable_elements.description: Timelines are defined for completion of medical record entries including admission notes, operative notes, and discharge summaries, and compliance is monitored.

objective_elements.description: Medical records contain all essential components including patient identification, clinical findings, treatment details, and informed consent.

measurable_elements.description: Every medical record includes patient demographics, clinical history, physical examination, investigation results, treatment plan, progress notes, consent forms, and discharge summary.

objective_elements.description: Access to medical records is restricted to authorized personnel with defined access levels based on role and need.

measurable_elements.description: A medical record access policy defines authorization levels, access request procedures, and tracking mechanisms to protect patient confidentiality.

objective_elements.description: A systematic storage and retrieval system ensures medical records are accessible when needed for patient care and other legitimate purposes.

measurable_elements.description: An organized filing and retrieval system with unique identification numbering, indexing, and tracking ensures timely availability of patient records.

objective_elements.description: A retention and safe destruction policy for medical records is defined in compliance with legal and regulatory requirements.

measurable_elements.description: Medical record retention periods comply with applicable laws, and a documented destruction process ensures confidentiality during disposal with proper authorization.

objective_elements.description: Regular audits of medical records are conducted to assess completeness, accuracy, and compliance with documentation standards.

measurable_elements.description: Periodic medical record audits are conducted using defined checklists, with results shared with clinical teams and improvement actions tracked.

standards.title: Clinical Data Management and Analysis
standards.description: The organization collects, analyzes, and utilizes clinical data to monitor care outcomes, support quality improvement, and enable evidence-based clinical practice.

objective_elements.description: Clinical data definitions and terminologies are standardized across the organization to ensure consistency in data capture.

measurable_elements.description: Standardized clinical terminologies, coding systems (ICD, procedure codes), and data dictionaries are adopted for consistent clinical data recording.

objective_elements.description: Clinical quality indicators are defined, monitored, and reported at regular intervals to track care quality and patient outcomes.

measurable_elements.description: A set of clinical quality indicators covering mortality, morbidity, readmission, and complication rates are tracked monthly and reported to quality committees.

objective_elements.description: Patient outcome data is systematically analyzed to identify trends, variations, and opportunities for clinical improvement.

measurable_elements.description: Patient outcome analysis including trend identification, variation analysis, and root cause evaluation is conducted and presented to clinical governance forums.

objective_elements.description: Adverse event and near-miss data is systematically captured, analyzed, and used to prevent recurrence.

measurable_elements.description: An adverse event and near-miss reporting database captures incident details, contributing factors, and corrective actions with trend analysis performed quarterly.

objective_elements.description: Infection surveillance data is collected, analyzed, and used to guide infection prevention and control strategies.

measurable_elements.description: Healthcare-associated infection rates are tracked by type and location, analyzed for trends, and reported to the infection control committee for targeted interventions.

objective_elements.description: Clinical performance is benchmarked against external standards, national databases, or peer institutions where available.

measurable_elements.description: Clinical outcomes are compared against national benchmarks, published standards, or peer institution data to identify performance gaps and best practices.

objective_elements.description: Data-driven clinical process improvements are initiated based on analysis of clinical indicators and outcome data.

measurable_elements.description: Clinical improvement projects with measurable objectives are initiated based on data analysis, with documented outcomes and sustainability plans.

standards.title: Information Technology Infrastructure
standards.description: The organization establishes and maintains adequate information technology infrastructure to support clinical operations, administrative functions, and data management needs.

objective_elements.description: An IT strategic plan aligned with organizational goals defines technology priorities, investments, and implementation timelines.

measurable_elements.description: A documented IT strategic plan with defined objectives, technology roadmap, budget allocation, and alignment to organizational strategy is approved and reviewed annually.

objective_elements.description: Adequate IT hardware, software, and network infrastructure is deployed to support clinical and administrative operations.

measurable_elements.description: IT infrastructure including servers, workstations, networking equipment, and licensed software is maintained in adequate quantities and kept current.

objective_elements.description: Network connectivity and system uptime are managed to ensure reliable access to information systems across the organization.

measurable_elements.description: Network uptime targets are defined and monitored, with redundancy measures and incident response procedures to minimize downtime impact on clinical operations.

objective_elements.description: An IT disaster recovery and business continuity plan ensures continuity of critical information systems during disruptions.

measurable_elements.description: A disaster recovery plan with defined RPO/RTO, backup strategies, failover mechanisms, and periodic testing is documented and maintained for critical systems.

objective_elements.description: IT helpdesk and technical support services are available to assist users and resolve technology issues promptly.

measurable_elements.description: An IT helpdesk with defined service hours, escalation matrix, response time targets, and ticket tracking system supports all organizational users.

objective_elements.description: Technology assessment and upgrade planning ensures that IT systems remain current, secure, and fit for purpose.

measurable_elements.description: Periodic technology assessments evaluate system performance, obsolescence risks, and user satisfaction to inform upgrade and replacement decisions.

standards.title: Data Security and Privacy
standards.description: The organization implements comprehensive data security and privacy measures to protect patient information and organizational data from unauthorized access, breaches, and loss.

objective_elements.description: A data security policy and framework is established to protect information assets from threats, vulnerabilities, and unauthorized access.

measurable_elements.description: A comprehensive information security policy covering asset classification, threat assessment, security controls, and incident management is approved and implemented.

objective_elements.description: Access controls and authentication mechanisms are implemented to restrict system and data access to authorized users only.

measurable_elements.description: Role-based access controls, strong authentication (passwords, multi-factor), and unique user IDs are implemented across all information systems with periodic access reviews.

objective_elements.description: Patient data confidentiality safeguards are implemented across physical, electronic, and verbal communication channels.

measurable_elements.description: Confidentiality safeguards including data encryption, screen privacy, document security, and verbal communication protocols are enforced across all care settings.

objective_elements.description: Cyber security measures including network protection, malware prevention, and monitoring are implemented to defend against digital threats.

measurable_elements.description: Cyber security controls including firewalls, intrusion detection, antivirus, patch management, and security monitoring are deployed and maintained across the network.

objective_elements.description: An incident response plan for data security breaches is documented, tested, and ready for activation.

measurable_elements.description: A data breach incident response plan with defined roles, communication protocols, containment steps, forensic procedures, and notification requirements is tested periodically.

objective_elements.description: Regular security audits and vulnerability assessments are conducted to identify and remediate security weaknesses.

measurable_elements.description: Security audits and vulnerability assessments are conducted at least annually or after significant changes, with findings documented and remediation tracked.

objective_elements.description: Staff training on data security awareness, privacy obligations, and safe data handling practices is conducted regularly.

measurable_elements.description: Mandatory data security awareness training covering phishing prevention, password hygiene, data handling, and privacy obligations is provided to all staff annually.

standards.title: Digital Personal Data Protection Compliance
standards.description: The organization ensures compliance with the Digital Personal Data Protection Act and other applicable data protection regulations governing the processing of personal health data.

objective_elements.description: A compliance framework for data protection regulations including the DPDP Act is established and integrated into organizational processes.

measurable_elements.description: A DPDP compliance framework mapping regulatory requirements to organizational processes, controls, and responsible persons is documented and maintained.

objective_elements.description: Consent management processes ensure lawful collection and processing of personal data with informed and verifiable consent.

measurable_elements.description: Consent collection processes for personal data processing are documented, include purpose specification, and provide mechanisms for consent withdrawal.

objective_elements.description: Processes are in place to address data principal rights including access, correction, erasure, and grievance redressal.

measurable_elements.description: Procedures for responding to data principal requests for access, correction, and erasure are defined with timelines and escalation paths.

objective_elements.description: Data processing activities are limited to specified purposes and conducted with transparency regarding data usage.

measurable_elements.description: A data processing register documents all categories of personal data processed, purposes, legal basis, retention periods, and sharing arrangements.

objective_elements.description: Data breach notification procedures comply with regulatory requirements for timely reporting to authorities and affected individuals.

measurable_elements.description: Data breach notification procedures define timelines, responsible persons, communication templates, and reporting channels for notifying the Data Protection Board and affected data principals.

objective_elements.description: A data protection officer or designated person is appointed to oversee data protection compliance and serve as the point of contact.

measurable_elements.description: A data protection officer or designated responsible person is formally appointed with defined responsibilities, authority, and independence to oversee DPDP compliance.

standards.title: Clinical Decision Support Systems
standards.description: The organization implements clinical decision support tools and systems to enhance evidence-based clinical practice and patient safety at the point of care.

objective_elements.description: Clinical decision support tools are available to assist clinicians in diagnosis, treatment planning, and care delivery.

measurable_elements.description: Clinical decision support tools such as clinical pathways, order sets, calculators, and reference databases are accessible to clinicians during patient care.

objective_elements.description: Drug interaction checking and allergy alert systems are implemented to prevent adverse medication events.

measurable_elements.description: Automated or manual drug-drug interaction checks and allergy alert mechanisms are in place at the prescribing and dispensing stages to prevent adverse events.

objective_elements.description: Evidence-based clinical guidelines and protocols are accessible to clinicians at the point of care.

measurable_elements.description: Current evidence-based clinical guidelines, protocols, and formulary information are accessible through electronic or physical means at patient care areas.

objective_elements.description: Clinical decision support is integrated with the electronic medical record system to provide context-aware alerts and recommendations.

measurable_elements.description: CDS tools are integrated with the EMR/HIS to provide real-time, patient-specific alerts, reminders, and evidence-based recommendations during clinical workflows.

objective_elements.description: The effectiveness and clinical impact of decision support tools are evaluated and improved based on user feedback and outcome data.

measurable_elements.description: Periodic evaluations of CDS tool effectiveness including alert compliance rates, override patterns, and impact on clinical outcomes are conducted with improvement actions.
